items.title: Retired civil servant, ex-temple chairman charged over RM50,000 forged invoice five years ago
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 6 — A retired civil servant and a former temple committee chairman were charged in separate Sessions Courts here today over a forged invoice for RM50,000 linked to consultancy services for temple repair works in the Petaling district five years ago. M Thiruselvam, 65, pleaded not guilty to the charge before Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin. He is accused of having dishonestly submitted the invoice for approval of repair works at a temple in Petaling, to a 64-year-old temple secretary on November 22, 2021, at the temple premises.

items.title: AirAsia X says no job cuts or unpaid leave despite industry pressures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X has assured that it is not implementing job cuts or voluntary unpaid leave as an option to manage rising costs due to the Middle East conflict. His comment comes days after reports that Batik Air Malaysia has offered voluntary unpaid leave to staff as part of its cost management efforts. Last week, Batik Air Malaysia said the airline will cut 35 per cent of its scheduled flights in the first half of April as well as offer voluntary unpaid leave to employees, with the applications open until April 3 for leave starting April 6.

items.title: Four 1MDB-linked paintings, including Picasso, yet to arrive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four high-value paintings, including a masterpiece by Pablo Picasso linked to the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, have yet to arrive in Malaysia. It is understood that the paintings will only be released following inspections by US authorities. The paintings identified for return are Pablo Picasso's L'Ecuyere et les clowns valued at US$26,250, Balthus' Etude pour femme couchee at US$28,125, Maurice Utrillo's Maison de rendez-vous de chasse de Henri IV, rue St Vincent, Montmartre at US$50,000, and Joan Miro's Composition at US$93,750.

items.title: Army corporal pleads not guilty to assaulting seven fellow soldiers in Bentong
items.summary: Sukry Tasu, 37, entered the plea before Magistrate Muhammad Aiman Azahan. On the first charge, he is accused of causing grievous hurt to Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, 24, at the armoury store of Company C, 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment Camp in Kem Bentong at 8.30am on March 30, 2026. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zamharir Muhammad Zuhid proposed bail of RM10,000, with an additional condition that the accused refrain from approaching or contacting the victim until the case is disposed of.

items.title: Petronas Kedai Mesra tops RM1bil for fourth straight year
items.summary: In its 2025 annual report, PetDag said non-fuel revenue was driven by continued expansion of its Kedai Mesra network and broader retail offerings. "In 2025, Mesra operated in a demanding environment shaped by tighter regulation and intensifying competition," Mesra Retail & Cafe Sdn Bhd chief executive officer Norazlan Subaha said. PetDag said non-fuel retail performance also reflected broader economic spillovers from higher mobility and consumer activity, resulting in stronger Kedai Mesra sales.

items.title: Bentong camp assault: Corporal pleads not guilty to injuring seven personnel
items.summary: Sukry Tasu, 37, entered the plea before Magistrate Muhammad Aiman Azahan. On the first charge, he is accused of causing grievous hurt to Muhammad Amirul Raziq Rosafindi, 24, at the armoury store of Company C, 25th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment Camp in Bentong camp at 8.30am on March 30, 2026. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Zamharir Muhammad Zuhid proposed bail of RM10,000, with an additional condition that the accused refrain from approaching or contacting the victim until the case is disposed of.

items.title: Malakoff breaks ground on Melaka waste-to-energy plant
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malakoff Corp Bhd has broken ground on its Sungai Udang waste-to-energy (WTE) plant in Melaka on April 2, marking progress in its efforts to expand its environmental solutions business while supporting Malaysia's shift towards more sustainable waste management. WTE projects are increasingly being positioned as an alternative to landfill disposal, particularly as existing sites face capacity constraints, while also contributing to energy generation from waste. The Sungai Udang plant is expected to help address Melaka's waste management needs as its current landfill nears full capacity, while enabling partial conversion of waste into electricity.

items.title: MMA: Scam targeting junior doctors at HTA reflects systemic failure
items.summary: The MMA's comments follow an incident at Tunku Azizah Hospital (HTA) involving 190 house officers, for which a police report has been lodged. MMA president Datuk Thirunavukarasu Rajoo, in a press statement today, said the case reflects a breakdown in administrative safeguards that placed junior doctors in a vulnerable position. He said MMA supports both the ongoing police investigation and a separate independent internal inquiry.

items.title: Sabah may review diesel subsidies if global energy crisis drags on, says Hajiji
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A prolonged global energy crisis could impact Sabah’s economy, including a possible review of diesel subsidies if current conditions persist. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the impact would depend on developments in global conflicts, particularly those involving energy-producing countries such as Iran, according to a report published in Sinar Harian today. He said if the energy crisis and ongoing conflicts continue, they would not only pressure global energy markets but also affect Sabah’s economy.

items.title: King, Queen welcome seventh grandchild
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, have welcomed their seventh grandchild. Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Dr Abdul Rahim Ramli announced that Her Highness Tunku Mahkota Johor Che Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Bustamam gave birth to a daughter on Sunday. The newborn is the fifth child of the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: Fresh charge for DOE deputy D-G over RM9,110 home repairs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 6 — Department of Environment (DOE) deputy director-general (Development) Dr Norhazni Mat Sari appeared in the Shah Alam Sessions Court today over a new charge of accepting RM9,110 for home repairs in 2022. Deputy public prosecutor Muaz Ahmad Khairuddin requested the court to maintain Norhazni’s bail granted by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court on April 3 and asked that this case be tried together with her previous charges, subject to the defence’s consent. Sessions Court Judge Datuk Mohd Nasir Nordin subsequently allowed the prosecution’s application for the case to be tried jointly and ordered that the bail and conditions imposed by the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court be maintained.

items.title: Asian stocks waver as Middle East developments send mixed signals
items.summary: HONG KONG: Equities in emerging Asia were mixed in holiday-thinned trading on Monday as investors grappled with a flurry of conflicting headlines around the war in the Middle East that continues to threaten inflation and economic outlooks. The MSCI gauge of EM Asia equities edged higher, largely driven by a gain of more than two per cent for South Korea's benchmark KOSPI index. A global EM currencies gauge was also marginally higher.

items.title: Jasbant: Karam Singh Walia was brother, mentor, icon in journalism
items.summary: KLANG: "I learnt a great deal from my brother," said Media Prima News and Current Affairs managing director Jasbant Singh, paying tribute to his late brother, Datuk Karam Singh Walia. Jasbant described Karam as both a personal mentor and a towering figure in Malaysia's journalism landscape, whose influence extended beyond the family. He was truly an icon of Malaysian journalism," Jasbant said when met at Karam's home here today.

items.title: Sabah orders capture, destruction of koi fish, non-endemic Cyprinidae
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The state Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Ministry has ordered the immediate capture and destruction of all koi fish and non-endemic Cyprinidae species from tagal areas, fish farms and rivers across Sabah. Jamawi said field investigations identified the primary cause of the outbreak as the uncontrolled release of foreign fish species, particularly koi and other non-endemic Cyprinidae, into public waters. He said such irresponsible actions violated biosecurity principles and caused serious infections among local fish species, including the high-value pelian.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia remains lower at midday amid cautious sentiment
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd rose two sen each to RM11.28 and RM14.06, respectively, SD Guthrie Bhd was flat at RM6.20, while Public Bank Bhd eased one sen to RM4.65 and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd declined five sen to RM7.44. On the most active list, VS Industry Bhd grew two sen to 20 sen, Velesto Energy Bhd added one sen to 36 sen, AmBest Holdings Bhd increased 5.5 sen to 40.5 sen, Zetrix AI Bhd was flat at 76 sen, and Top Glove Corp Bhd shed 4.5 sen to 76.5 sen. As for top losers, Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd dropped 50 sen to RM22.38, Petronas Dagangan Bhd lost 42 sen to RM20.80, Hong Leong Bank Bhd contracted 34 sen to RM21.38, and IHH Healthcare Bhd erased 24 sen to RM8.72.

items.title: MCA to accord highest honour to Ling Liong Sik with coffin flag-draping ceremony tomorrow, says Wee Ka Siong
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A special ceremony will be held tomorrow to drape the coffin of the late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik with the MCA party flag, an honour described as the highest tribute accorded to a leader of the party. MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong said the ceremony will take place at 4pm at the Xiao En Centre in Cheras, where family members, party leaders and members of the public have been paying their final respects to the former party president and transport minister. MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong with MCA party leaders at the wake of late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik at at Xiao En Centre, Kuala Lumpur, on April 6, 2026.

items.title: Geohan bags RM103mil subcontracts under IJM's New Pantai Expressway extension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Geohan Corp Bhd has secured two subcontract packages worth a combined RM103 million for works under the New Pantai Highway Extension project. Its wholly-owned Geohan Sdn Bhd was awarded the contracts by IJM Construction Sdn Bhd. The first package, valued at RM27.4 million, involves hand-dug caisson piling works and comprehensive pile testing, while the second, worth RM75.6 million, covers bored piling and reverse circulation drilling works.

items.title: Vietnam parliament chairman Tran Thanh Man reelected for second term
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam's National Assembly on Monday unanimously re-elected Tran Thanh Man as chairman for a second term, state media reported. Man, 63, has held the post since May 2024. --REUTERS © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MoE orders all Teacher Education Institute campuses to suspend water, adventure activities after two students drown
items.summary: IPOH, April 6 — The Ministry of Education (MoE) has ordered all Teacher Education Institute (IPG) campuses to temporarily suspend water and adventure activities following a drowning incident involving two students from the institute. Last Saturday, two students from IPG Perlis, Christopher Ling Jia Siang, 21, and Joey Ling Lin Siang, 20, were found drowned at about 7.30pm while participating in kayaking activities at Pantai Merdeka near Sungai Petani. “Police investigations are ongoing, and the MoE’s internal review is also underway.

items.title: 'Not the Cairo we know': Energy shock from Iran war dims Egypt nights
items.summary: AT a roadside cafe in downtown Cairo, Abu Ali was mid domino throw when the lights snapped off under new early-closing orders enacted to curb Egypt's soaring energy bill due to the US-Israel war on Iran. "I used to stay here until 2am," the 63-year-old told AFP, tapping his tiles as the street went dark. "This is usually when work starts," shopworker Ali Haggag said, standing outside his suddenly quiet clothing store.

items.title: Former civil servant, friend charged with smuggling 77 foreigners in lorry [WATCH]
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: A former civil servant and his friend were charged in the Sessions Court with smuggling 77 Myanmar and Bangladeshi migrants in a lorry last month. Muhammad Izzuddin Mustafa, 38, and Aliff Ezry Ismail, 35, nodded when the charges against them were read before judge N. Priscilla Hemamalini. Aliff Ezry was unrepresented.

items.title: Kedah Customs intercept RM3.9m worth of ecstasy and meth
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 6 — The Kedah Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) seized over 46kg of drugs during an operation on Jalan Kota Kuala Muda near Sungai Petani on February 21. State JKDM director Wan Marini Wan Hamzah said officers from the Langkawi and Tanjung Dawai Marine Enforcement Branches, acting on three months of intelligence and surveillance, tracked a sedan driven by a 40-year-old Indonesian man suspected of transporting illegal substances. The drugs were found in bags on the rear seat of the car,” he said during a press conference at the JKDM office here today.

items.title: Govt rules out permits for KL ‘photo touts’, citing safety risks, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The government will not issue special permits to the ulat foto (photo touts) operating at tourist hotspots in Kuala Lumpur, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said. She said safety concerns, particularly for road users, were behind the decision not to compromise on such activities. Yeoh said joint enforcement operations between the police and DBKL had helped curb the activity, which she said was largely carried out by foreigners using false identities.

items.title: Iran escalates attacks after Trump's 'hell' warning
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran launched fresh attacks across the Middle East on Monday and threatened "devastating" retaliation, after US President Donald Trump's expletive-laced warning that Teheran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates reported a barrage of missile and drone strikes early Monday. The attacks came as Teheran ramped up rhetoric against Trump, with Teheran's central military command warning of "much more devastating" retaliation if its adversaries hit civilian targets.

items.title: Putrajaya youth get global edge with new international language classes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — Perbadanan Putrajaya (PPj) is stepping up efforts to develop a competitive younger generation by introducing international language classes for children in Putrajaya, in line with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call for Malaysians to master global languages. “These language classes are part of Carsome’s corporate social responsibility initiative, following their establishment in Putrajaya in January. “I hope other private companies will also support similar initiatives,” she said after attending the Putrajaya International Language Initiative for Youth and Special Community Mobility Assistance programme today.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Knowledge, innovation are Malaysia’s new ‘jihad’ for progress
items.summary: KUCHING, April 6 — Mastering knowledge and fostering an innovative society are forms of “jihad”, or struggle, in driving national progress and strengthening the economy in line with the Malaysia Madani vision, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also minister of energy transition and water transformation, said the ongoing crisis in West Asia has highlighted key sectors with strong potential for development, including food security, finance and energy. He noted that Malaysia still relies heavily on imports, particularly in food supply.

items.title: South Korea's Lee expresses regret to North Korea over drone incursion
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung on Monday expressed regret to North Korea over a drone incursion that he said was carried out by a South Korean individual without government approval, stressing Seoul's commitment to preventing future incidents. Lee said an investigation by South Korean authorities found a National Intelligence Service (NIS) employee and an active-duty military official were involved in the case, adding that the constitution forbade individual acts of provocation against North Korea. North Korea in recent months has declared South Korea as its most hostile state and rebuffed outreach efforts by Lee's administration.

items.title: Rayer: Why no action in alleged Balik Pulau assault caught on CCTV?
items.summary: He said the woman was attacked by a man in front of her shop along Jalan Tun Sardon, in an incident clearly captured on CCTV. “She suffered bruises on her left and right hands after the man kicked her,” he said at a press conference at DAP headquarters here today. He said she lodged a police report on the incident but alleged that no action was taken.

items.title: Karam Singh Walia set benchmark few could match
items.summary: KLANG: The late Datuk Karam Singh Walia, known for his discipline and exacting standards, set a benchmark in broadcast journalism that few have matched. Media Prima On-Air Presentation, News and Current Affairs Division general manager Kamaruddin Mape said Karam consistently prioritised quality and precision in every assignment throughout his career. Kamaruddin, who sat next to Karam in the newsroom, said they shared many memories over the years.

items.title: UOB, PAAB launch vendor financing scheme for water infra supply chain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: UOB Malaysia and Pengurusan Aset Air Bhd (PAAB), a wholly-owned company of the Minister of Finance (Incorporated), have launched a scheme for suppliers and vendors within PAAB's supply chain ecosystem. The UOB-PAAB Vendor Financing Programme offers financing to PAAB vendors participating in the Supplier-Financing Irrevocable Payment Undertaking (SF-IPU) facility. Anchored on PAAB's project payment undertaking, the programme offers competitive non-recourse financing designed to help vendors manage cash flow, reduce financial constraints and maintain operational continuity.

items.title: 'Flying kick' on woman caught on video: Rayer questions NFA decision
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Jelutong member of parliament RSN Rayer has raised concerns over the lack of prosecution in an alleged assault case involving a woman, despite video evidence and a medical report confirming her injuries. Rayer said a police report was lodged by the complainant, Fam Yean Nie, 39, on March 4, following an incident in a public place that was captured on video. Rayer said the complainant sustained injuries, with a medical report indicating a soft tissue injury to her right hand, although no fractures were detected.

items.title: SME group Samenta gives Steven Sim’s first 100 days an ‘A’ — but calls for deeper reforms
items.summary: Samenta president William Ng said the minister’s ‘Hebatkan Perniagaan Malaysia’ campaign had quickly channelled RM2 billion in financing to more than 88,000 micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), providing timely relief amid economic uncertainty. “For this proactive ‘first-aid’ to our business ecosystem, Samenta gives the Minister and the Ministry a solid ‘A’,” Ng said, praising the government’s ability to mobilise resources quickly to support businesses. However, he said the government must go further if it hopes to deliver lasting change.

items.title: Smart Energy Mission with Ejen Ali x Jero [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Join Ejen Ali and Jero as they explore practical energy efficiency tips for everyday living. Amid increasingly unpredictable and hot weather, adopting smarter energy habits becomes increasingly important, not only to stay comfortable, but also to ensure energy is used more efficiently. For more information, please visit https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hVnQc9Emdog © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Higher Education innovation can turn crisis into opportunity
items.summary: LETTERS: Rising fuel prices present opportunities for reform and innovation within the higher education sector. Investments in renewable energy, such as installing solar panels on campus buildings, adopting smart energy management systems, and implementing energy-efficient building designs, can reduce dependence on conventional energy sources. These may include special grants for energy efficiency initiatives, tax incentives for investments in green technologies, and expanded financial assistance for students affected by rising living costs.

items.title: Court stay on 40pct federal revenue dispute is procedural, says Sabah Law Society
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Sabah Law Society (SLS) has described the Court of Appeal's decision to grant a stay in the Sabah 40 per cent revenue dispute as procedural in nature. Earlier, a three-member appellate court bench led by Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan allowed the federal government's application for a stay of execution of a High Court order requiring a review of Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement. The court also satisfied that special circumstances existed, particularly given the constraints imposed by the High Court's timelines.

items.title: Daim’s daughter appears at MACC over asset probe, siblings yet to be seen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Asnida Abdul Daim, a daughter of former finance minister the late Tun Daim Zainuddin, arrived at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters in Putrajaya this morning to give a statement in an ongoing asset probe. Her appearance is tied to an investigation into the alleged failure to comply with two official notices requiring asset declarations. As of this morning, her three siblings — Datuk Md Wira Dani, Muhammed Amir Zainuddin and Muhammed Amin Zainuddin — had yet to be seen.

items.title: Sabah Law Society says Putrajaya stay ‘procedural’ in 40pc revenue case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — The Sabah Law Society (SLS) today said the Court of Appeal’s decision to grant a stay of execution in the Sabah 40 per cent revenue dispute is procedural in nature and does not resolve the substantive constitutional issues at the centre of the case. It does not displace the findings of the High Court,” its president Datuk Mohamed Nazim Maduarin said in a statement. The Court of Appeal earlier allowed Putrajaya’s application for a stay, pausing both negotiations and payment deadlines imposed by the High Court while the federal government pursues its appeal.

items.title: AirAsia X committed to opening Bahrain hub despite Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Budget carrier AirAsia X said on Monday that it remained set on opening a planned hub in Bahrain in June, though the airline's chief executive was non-committal on whether the plans would go ahead if the Middle East conflict drags on. AirAsia X in February unveiled plans to resume flights from Kuala Lumpur to London via the Bahrain hub, its first outside Asia, with services due to begin on June 26. AirAsia X chief executive Bo Lingam told a press conference that the Bahrain service would definitely go ahead if the war ends before June, but declined to say if it would proceed in the event of a protracted conflict.

items.title: Fishermen’s fuel quota under govt review as costs bite
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security is reviewing proposals to improve the subsidised fuel quota for fishermen following concerns raised by the fishing community over the 50-litre daily limit for Zone A vessels. The Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM) in a statement today said any proposals to improve the rate or mechanism of the subsidy will be examined in stages, taking into consideration the welfare of fishermen as well as the country’s financial capacity. It said the government understands the challenges faced by fishermen, particularly on the rising operating costs and technological changes in vessels that affect fuel consumption, but at the same time urging them to understand the current global energy crisis, which has had a direct impact on fuel prices and operating costs.

items.title: Singapore, Brunei firms win RM1.65bil 'green' fuel project in Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore-based Oiltek International Ltd and Brunei's BioSeaga Industries Sdn Bhd have agreed to collaborate on a large-scale sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) biorefinery in Sabah. Oiltek said the planned facility will use palm oil mill effluent and used cooking oil as its main feedstocks. Oiltek Sdn Bhd (Oiltek Malaysia), a subsidiary of Oiltek, has been appointed as the exclusive EPCC partner for the plant's pre-treatment facilities, SAF production plant and tank farm and logistics bulking infrastructure.

items.title: Singapore's Olam to sell IT unit to Wipro for US$375 million
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore-based Olam Group said on Monday it has agreed to sell its IT and digital services business, Mindsprint, to Indian IT services firm Wipro for an enterprise value of US$375 million. Olam Holdings, an unit of the Singapore-based food and agri-business conglomerate, will sell 200 million ordinary shares of Mindsprint to Wipro Networks, a Wipro subsidiary. Separately, OG Group has awarded Wipro an eight-year services contract with a committed annual spend of US$100 million.

items.title: Loke orders crackdown on Mat Rempit at Kelantan airport
items.summary: KOTA BHARU: Transport Minister Anthony Loke has slammed a group of mat rempit for turning the departure hall area of the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal into a racetrack, describing the act as disgraceful and shameful. "The behaviour of these mat rempit in front of the new terminal departure hall not only endangers the public but can also be described as an act of sabotage against the safety and economy of the state." © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Targeted subsidies, fiscal discipline lift investor confidence, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global investors are piling into Malaysian bonds, attracted by the country's ability to manage oil price volatility through targeted subsidies and disciplined fiscal policy, Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said. Amir Hamzah said the inflows reflect growing investor confidence in Malaysia's ability to navigate energy shocks without destabilising the economy. While Malaysia is a net energy exporter, Amir Hamzah said the country remains exposed to imports when oil prices surge, but has managed that exposure more effectively than its regional peers.

items.title: Gold falls on firm dollar, fading Fed rate-cut hopes
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell more than one per cent on Monday, pressured by a stronger dollar and fading hopes of US Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. Spot gold fell 1.2 per cent to US$4,620.68 per ounce by 0047 GMT. US gold futures for April delivery lost 0.7 per cent to US$4,647.10.

items.title: US, Iran and mediators make push for 45-day ceasefire
items.summary: NEW YORK: The US, Iran and a group of regional mediators are discussing the terms for a potential 45-day ceasefire that could lead to a permanent end to the war, Axios reported on Sunday, citing four US, Israeli and regional sources with knowledge of the talks. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. The second phase would be an agreement on ending the war, the report said.

items.title: King grants audience to chief secretary to govt at Istana Pasir Pelangi in JB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 6 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. The audience took place at Istana Pasir Pelangi here. Also present was Public-Private Partnership Unit (UKAS) director-general Datuk Muhammad Razman Abu Samah.

items.title: Have you seen her? N. Sembilan police search for missing Bahau woman, 50
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 6 — Police are seeking public assistance to look for a missing woman whose family members have been unable to contact her since yesterday. Jempol district police chief Supt Norhisham Mustapar said they received a missing person’s report on Norizan Yahya, 50, at 8.46am lodged by her sister, who saw her last at 7.30am yesterday. Those with information or have come across the woman can contact the Bahau police station at 06-4541222 or Bahau police station chief, Insp Annas Fikri Othman at 013-6219196.

items.title: Authorities round up 41 undocumented migrants in Kelantan and Kedah operations
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 6 — Authorities detained 41 undocumented migrants in separate operations in Kelantan and Kedah, highlighting continued enforcement efforts against illegal entry and employment of foreign nationals. In Kelantan, 20 Myanmar nationals were believed to have paid about RM8,000 each to agents for a journey from Myanmar into Malaysia via illegal routes for work as labourers. “Further checks found that all suspects failed to produce valid travel documents and are believed to have entered the country through illegal routes from Thailand,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Nuclear energy eyed as part of Malaysia’s future power mix, says minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 6 — Nuclear energy is gaining attention as a potential component of Malaysia’s energy mix amid rising demand and the need to transition to low-carbon sources, Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Chang Lih Kang said. He said Malaysia is comprehensively assessing the potential of nuclear technology in addressing future energy needs. Chang said the approach is clear, with nuclear energy not intended to replace existing sources such as solar, hydro or gas, but to complement them in ensuring a stable, sustainable and resilient long-term energy supply.

items.title: RM200,000 upgrade at Greentown Utama Food Court boosts comfort for 25 Perak traders, says minister
items.summary: IPOH, April 6 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) remains committed to enhancing public amenities through the implementation of infrastructure upgrading projects at the Greentown Utama Food Court here. He said the project is part of a RM60 million allocation for the implementation of 200 food court upgrading projects nationwide, which are expected to be fully completed by the end of this year. In addition, Nga said road resurfacing works in residential areas across Perak worth RM40 million have been fully completed.

items.title: Court of Appeal grants stay to Putrajaya over Sabah 40pc revenue ruling
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya has granted the federal government a stay of execution on a High Court order tied to Sabah’s long-contested 40 per cent share of net federal revenue, pausing both negotiations and any payment deadlines while Putrajaya pursues its appeal. Delivering the ruling, as reported by FMT , judge Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan said the government would “clearly be prejudiced” if compelled to comply before its appeal is heard, particularly given the scale and timing of the obligations imposed. “We also note that the court’s order not only directs negotiations, but also requires payment from the federal consolidated fund within 180 days of the High Court’s order,” he said.

items.title: Viral wrong-way driver on Old Klang Road surrenders, says he lost his way to Mid Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A driver who sparked alarm after being filmed driving against traffic along Jalan Klang Lama in Kuala Lumpur has surrendered to police and was arrested last night. The incident drew attention after a video showing a car being driven against traffic along Jalan Klang Lama went viral yesterday. Checks later established that the incident occurred at around 9.44am, along a roughly 500m stretch between the Jalan Klang Lama–Jalan Sepadu junction and the Jalan Klang Lama–Jalan Kuchai Lama junction.

items.title: Melaka F&B firms retain licences despite halal certification revocation, says exco
items.summary: MELAKA, April 6 — The business licences of two food and beverage (F&B) companies in Melaka will not be revoked, despite the Malaysian Halal Certification (SPHM) for both companies being withdrawn over the alleged use of pig-bristle brushes for cleaning equipment. State Senior Exco for Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change, and Disaster Management, Datuk Rais Yasin, said both companies were not found to have breached any business licence conditions issued by the Melaka Historic City Council (MBMB). Also in attendance was Melaka Historic City Council Mayor, Datuk Shadan Othman.

items.title: Masked group in lorry rams gate, vandalises Cheras entertainment centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — A group of masked individuals broke into an entertainment centre in Cheras and damaged property inside the premises in an incident believed to have occurred at about 1.30pm yesterday-. Kuala Lumpur police chief Fadil Marsus said police received a report on the incident at 7.30pm. Earlier, a 71-second video went viral showing a group of masked individuals breaking into an entertainment centre.

items.title: Driver held after road rage attack leaves motorcyclist injured in Kulai
items.summary: Police said the altercation occurred at about 5.45pm as the 37-year-old victim was travelling to work in Singapore. Initial investigations found the suspect had been driving recklessly and suddenly changed lanes, nearly colliding with the victim’s motorcycle. The victim sustained minor injuries.

items.title: Hot, dry weather fuels three Pasir Mas bushfires, swiftly contained by fire crews
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 6 — Three bushfires, two in Tasek Bakong and one in Banggol Kulim, Pasir Mas, were extinguished yesterday by personnel from the Rantau Panjang Fire and Rescue Station (BBP). “Two fires were reported in Kampung Tasek Bakong, affecting an area of about 1.6 hectares. The team then proceeded to Kampung Banggol Kulim to extinguish another fire involving about 0.8 hectares,” he said when contacted.

items.title: Former TV3 journalist Karam Singh Walia dies at 67
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Veteran broadcast journalist Datuk Karam Singh Walia died at his residence in Klang at 3.30am today. His passing was confirmed by his younger brother, Jasbant Singh, who is also Group Managing Editor (GME) of News and Current Affairs at Media Prima Berhad. “Karam Singh Walia was not just a journalist.

items.title: From Monet to millions: Over RM150m in assets linked to ex-1MDB lawyer Jasmine Loo traced in Switzerland, MACC says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 6 — Assets linked to former 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) lawyer Jasmine Loo in Switzerland are estimated to exceed RM150 million, spanning holdings in US dollars and euros. The haul includes four of 12 high-value paintings by world-renowned artists tied to the misappropriation of 1MDB funds, collectively valued at more than US$30 million (RM120 million). According to Utusan Malaysia , Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) special operations division senior director Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin said US$9.26 million had been traced to several accounts and other holdings.

items.title: Sibu police seize RM164,695 worth of drugs, Indonesian man arrested
items.summary: SIBU, April 6 — Police arrested an Indonesian man and seized various drugs worth RM164,695 in an integrated operation around the town over the weekend. Sibu police chief ACP Azari Miskon said the 25-year-old was detained at about 11.30 pm yesterday along Jalan Padawan, with 272.02 grammes of powder suspected to be ecstasy and 2.78 grammes of ketamine seized. “The suspect then led police to a house in Lorong Kiew Nang, where a further 1,089.79 grammes of ketamine, 4,509.65 grammes of powder suspected to be ecstasy, 278.51 grammes of Erimin 5 (890 tablets) and 140 millilitres of liquid believed to be ecstasy were seized,” he said.

items.title: Terengganu retired teacher duped of RM165,900 in police phone scam
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 6 — A retired teacher lost RM165,900 after falling victim to a phone scam syndicate. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 64-year-old woman received a call on November 23 last year from individuals posing as police officers and prosecutors. The suspects falsely claimed that the victim, from Kuala Terengganu, was involved in money laundering and would face legal action.
